title: Build a Beat
description: Create a drum pattern using the step sequencer and real-time recording.

steps.title: Select a Pattern
steps.instruction: Turn the pattern knob to select an empty pattern slot. The display shows the current pattern number (1-16).
steps.tip: Pattern 1 is often used for your main beat. Try using pattern 16 for experiments.

steps.title: Set the Tempo
steps.instruction: Turn the BPM knob to set your desired tempo. The display shows the current BPM (60-240).
steps.tip: 90-100 BPM is good for hip-hop, 120-130 for house music.

steps.title: Enter Write Mode
steps.instruction: Press the write button to enter step-sequencing mode. The display will show the current step number.

steps.title: Add Kick Drums
steps.instruction: Turn the sound knob to step 1. Press pad 9 (or wherever your kick is) to add a kick. Turn to step 5, 9, and 13 and add kicks for a four-on-the-floor pattern.

substeps.instruction: Turn sound knob to step 1, press kick pad
Turn to step 5, press kick pad
Turn to step 9, press kick pad
Turn to step 13, press kick pad

steps.tip: The sound knob navigates steps in write mode (1-16).

steps.title: Add Snares
steps.instruction: Turn sound knob to step 5, press your snare pad. Turn to step 13, press snare again. This places snares on beats 2 and 4.
steps.tip: Multiple sounds can be placed on the same step for layering.

steps.title: Add Hi-Hats
steps.instruction: Add hi-hats on every other step (1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15) or every step for a busier feel. Navigate with the sound knob, press your hi-hat pad.

steps.title: Exit and Play
steps.instruction: Press write to exit write mode. Press play to hear your beat! Make adjustments by entering write mode again.
steps.tip: Hold write + press a pad to delete that sound from all steps if you make a mistake.

outcomes: Ability to place sounds on specific steps
